session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Erk Glossary 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "understanding project terminology" 7→ - "confused about domain-specific terms" 8→ - "working with worktrees, plans, or stacks" 9→ - "working with objectives or turns" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "parsing objective roadmap PR column status" 12→ warning: "Roadmap table uses separate Plan and PR columns: Plan `#XXXX`=in progress (plan PR), PR `#XXXX`=done (merged PR), both empty=pending. Legacy 4-col format with `plan #XXXX` in PR column is auto-migrated." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Erk Glossary 16→ 17→Definitive terminology reference for the erk project. 18→ 19→**Purpose**: Eliminate confusion about domain-specific terms. When in doubt about terminology, consult this document. 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Core Concepts 24→ 25→### activate.sh 26→ 27→A shell script at `.erk/activate.sh` in each worktree that sets up the development environment when sourced. 28→ 29→**Purpose**: Opt-in shell integration. Users explicitly source this script rather than relying on automatic shell manipulation. 30→
... [1017 lines omitted] ...
543→A sentinel class returned when a PR lookup fails, preserving lookup context (branch name, PR number). Uses `isinstance` check (LBYL) instead of exceptions. 564→- `style: Literal["info", "success", "warning", "error"]` - Visual style hint (default: "info") 716→- `tripwires`: List of action-triggered warnings 727→ warning: "Use .erk/scratch/<session-id>/ instead." 754→**Format**: Defined in doc frontmatter with `action` (pattern to detect) and `warning` (guidance message). 763→ warning: "AI workflow files belong in .erk/scratch/<session-id>/, NOT /tmp/." 841→- `erk land` skips the "not learned from" warning for learn plans (they don't need learning) 947→- **EAFP**: Easier to Ask for Forgiveness than Permission (exception-based error handling) 948→- **LBYL**: Look Before You Leap (check-before-operation error handling) 1012→**Purpose**: Break large, complex goals into reviewable, implementable chunks. Instead of tackling "migrate all errors to Ensure class" in one massive PR, an objective evaluates the codebase, identifies a manageable subset of violations, and creates a plan for that subset.

1→--- 2→title: CLI Output Styling Guide 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "styling CLI output" 7→ - "using colors in CLI" 8→ - "formatting terminal output" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "using click.confirm() after user_output()" 11→ warning: "Use ctx.console.confirm() for testability, or user_confirm() if no context available. Direct click.confirm() after user_output() causes buffering hangs because stderr isn't flushed." 12→ - action: "displaying user-provided text in Rich CLI tables" 13→ warning: "Use `escape_markup(value)` for user data. Brackets like `[text]` are interpreted as Rich style tags and will disappear." 14→ - action: "writing multi-line error messages in Ensure method calls" 15→ warning: "Use implicit string concatenation with \n at end of first string. Line 1 is the primary error, line 2+ is remediation context. Do NOT use \n\n (double newline) — Ensure handles spacing." 16→ - action: "using Python format strings with :N width specifiers for CLI output containing emoji" 17→ warning: "Use Rich tables instead — emoji have variable terminal widths (typically 2 cells) which break fixed-width alignment. See the Rich Tables for Variable-Width Characters section below." 18→--- 19→ 20→# CLI Output Styling Guide 21→ 22→This guide defines the standard color scheme, emoji conventions, and output abstraction patterns for erk CLI commands. 23→ 24→## Color Conventions 25→ 26→Use consistent colors and styling for CLI output via `click.style()`: 27→ 28→| Element | Color | Bold | Example | 29→| ------------------------ | ---------------- | ---- | --------------------------------------------------- | 30→| Branch names | `yellow` | No | `click.style(branch, fg="yellow")` |
... [934 lines omitted] ...
39→| Error states | `red` | No | `click.style("Error", fg="red")` | 114→- The command is long/complex and manual copying would be error-prone 137→- Unsupported terminals silently ignore the sequence (no errors) 151→- `❌` - Errors/failures 378→| Error messages | `user_output()` | User info, goes to stderr | 392→user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Branch not found") 409→user_output("Warning: This operation is destructive!") 422→user_output("Warning: This operation is destructive!") 434→user_output("Warning: This operation is destructive!") 444→- `src/erk/cli/commands/stack/consolidate_cmd.py` - Uses user_output() for error messages 446→## Error Message Guidelines 448→Use the `Ensure` class (from `erk.cli.ensure`) for all CLI validation errors. This provides consistent error styling and messaging. 450→### Error Message Format 452→All error messages should follow these principles: 465→**DO NOT** include "Error: " prefix - the `Ensure` class adds it automatically in red. 467→### Multi-line Error Messages 469→For errors that need both a primary message and remediation context, use implicit string concatenation with `
`: 481→- Line 1: Primary error description (concise, specific) 485→**DO NOT** use `

` (double newline) — the Ensure class already handles spacing in its error output formatting. 487→This pattern was established in the admin.py migration (PR #6860) and applies to all Ensure method calls where the error benefits from remediation context. 493→| `"Configuration file not found at ~/.erk/config.yml - Run 'erk init' to create it"` | `"Error: Config missing"` | 494→| `"Worktree already exists at path {path} - Use --force to overwrite or choose a different name"` | `"Error: Path exists"` | 496→| `"No child branches found - Already at the top of the stack"` | `"Validation failed"` | 500→| Situation | Error Message Template | 508→### UserFacingCliError for Mid-Function Errors 510→For errors that occur mid-function where `Ensure` precondition checks don't fit, use `UserFacingCliError`: 513→from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError 517→if isinstance(push_result, PushError): 518→ raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message) 523→- **Mid-logic errors**: Errors discovered during execution, not preconditions 524→- **Discriminated union errors**: When consuming `Result | Error` types from gateway layer 525→- **After operations fail**: When an operation returns an error variant 530→- `UserFacingCliError`: For errors during execution (e.g., git push fails) 531→- Both produce the same styled output (`Error: ` prefix in red) 533→- Ensure uses `UserFacingCliError` internally 538→- Mid-function operation errors → Raise `UserFacingCliError` 539→- Complex multi-step operations → Mix both (Ensure upfront, UserFacingCliError for failures) 575→For multi-step validations, use sequential Ensure calls with specific error messages: 578→# Multi-condition validation - each check has specific error 591→# Validation with early return - fail fast on first error 597→**Design Principle:** Prefer simple sequential checks over complex validation abstractions. Each check should have a specific, actionable error message. This aligns with the LBYL (Look Before You Leap) philosophy and makes code easier to understand and debug. 599→**Exit Codes:** All Ensure methods use exit code 1 for validation failures. This is consistent across all CLI commands. 607→1. **If error has a boolean condition** → Use `Ensure.invariant()` 612→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Something is wrong") 619→2. **If error returns a value when truthy** → Use `Ensure.truthy()` 625→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "No results found") 632→3. **If error checks for None specifically** → Use `Ensure.not_none()` 638→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Value is required") 645→4. **If error has no clear condition or needs custom flow** → Keep as direct pattern 649→**Pattern: Fallthrough/catch-all errors with no clear boolean condition** 651→Some errors occur as the "else" case after multiple checks have been exhausted. There's no meaningful boolean condition to express - the error state IS the remaining case. 664→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") 670→**Why not migrate:** Using `Ensure.invariant(True, ...)` or wrapping with an artificial condition would be misleading. The error isn't about a condition being false - it's about reaching a catch-all state. 672→**Pattern: Errors with complex multi-line remediation messages** 674→When the error message spans multiple lines with detailed instructions, the `Ensure` API may not accommodate the formatting needs cleanly. 676→**Pattern: Errors that need conditional additional output before exit** 684→1. **Identify the error condition** - Is there a clear boolean/truthy/None check? 686→3. **Write the error message** - Follow the Error Message Guidelines (no "Error: " prefix) 687→4. **Test behavior is unchanged** - Error should trigger at the same conditions 697→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Already at the top...") 711→- The error message is a single line 755→| Action: Failed | `[red]Failed[/red]` | Execution failed | 850→This causes columns to appear misaligned whenever emoji are present. The misalignment is silent — code runs without errors but output looks wrong.
session.assistant: Now I need to read the remaining files. Let me continue with the capabilities, architecture, command, and skill files:
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Capabilities Documentation 5→ 6→- **[adding-new-capabilities.md](adding-new-capabilities.md)** — adding reminder capabilities, creating new capability types, debugging capability registration, understanding why capabilities don't appear in hooks 7→- **[adding-reviews.md](adding-reviews.md)** — adding review capabilities, creating code review definitions, understanding ReviewCapability pattern 8→- **[adding-skills.md](adding-skills.md)** — adding skill capabilities, creating new skills for external projects, understanding SkillCapability pattern 9→- **[adding-workflows.md](adding-workflows.md)** — adding workflow capabilities, creating GitHub Actions workflow capabilities, understanding workflow capability pattern 10→- **[bundled-skills.md](bundled-skills.md)** — registering new bundled skills in the capability system, understanding how skills are discovered by erk init and erk artifact sync, debugging missing skill capabilities 11→- **[folder-structure.md](folder-structure.md)** — adding new capability implementations, deciding where to place capability files, understanding capability organization 12→- **[learned-docs-capability.md](learned-docs-capability.md)** — modifying the learned-docs capability install or uninstall logic, debugging partial learned-docs installation, understanding how learned-docs artifacts are bundled in the wheel, adding new artifacts to the learned-docs capability 13→- **[npx-skill-management.md](npx-skill-management.md)** — adding a new skill to erk, migrating a bundled skill to npx distribution, understanding how skills-lock.json works, debugging skill installation issues, removing a skill from bundled distribution 14→- **[skill-deletion-patterns.md](skill-deletion-patterns.md)** — deleting a skill from erk, understanding tombstone pattern for skills, working with erk-planning tombstone, removing a skill from bundled_skills registry 15→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1→--- 2→title: Skill Deletion Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "deleting a skill from erk" 5→ - "understanding tombstone pattern for skills" 6→ - "working with erk-planning tombstone" 7→ - "removing a skill from bundled_skills registry" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "completely removing a skill's SKILL.md without leaving a tombstone" 10→ warning: "When erk syncs skills to external repos, it only installs — never removes. A stale skill from a previous installation will remain active in external repos unless a tombstone SKILL.md overwrites it. Always leave a tombstone." 11→--- 12→ 13→# Skill Deletion Patterns 14→ 15→When a skill is deleted from erk, it must be replaced with a tombstone to ensure external repos that previously installed the skill receive the deletion on next sync. 16→ 17→## Why Tombstones? 18→ 19→Erk syncs skills to external repos via `erk capability install`. This is an additive operation — it copies skills but never deletes them. If you completely remove a skill's files, external repos that previously installed it will continue using the stale version indefinitely. 20→ 21→A tombstone overwrites the stale installation: on next sync, external repos receive the tombstone SKILL.md which displays the deletion message and points users to the replacement. 22→ 23→## Tombstone Pattern 24→ 25→Replace the skill's `SKILL.md` with a minimal tombstone: 26→ 27→```markdown 28→--- 29→name: skill-name 30→description: "[REMOVED] This skill has been deleted. <Replacement instructions>"
... [77 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: GitHub GraphQL Label Semantics 3→read_when: 4→ - "querying GitHub issues or PRs with multiple label filters" 5→ - "debugging unexpected empty results from GitHub GraphQL label queries" 6→ - "implementing label-based filtering for plan or issue views" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "passing multiple labels to a GitHub GraphQL label filter expecting OR semantics" 9→ warning: "GitHub GraphQL uses AND semantics for label filters. Passing labels=['erk-plan', 'erk-learn'] returns only items with BOTH labels, not either. Query by type-specific labels separately." 10→ - action: "querying plans by base label erk-planned-pr instead of type-specific labels" 11→ warning: "Query by type-specific labels (erk-plan, erk-learn) not base label. AND semantics means querying erk-planned-pr + erk-plan returns only items with both, which may silently exclude items." 12→--- 13→ 14→# GitHub GraphQL Label Semantics 15→ 16→GitHub's GraphQL API uses AND semantics for label filter arrays — not OR. This is undocumented and causes silent data loss when queries expect OR behavior. 17→ 18→## The Problem 19→ 20→When you pass `labels: ["erk-plan", "erk-learn"]` to a GitHub GraphQL query, GitHub returns only issues that have **both** labels, not issues with **either** label. This is the opposite of what most developers expect. 21→ 22→## Silent Failure Mode 23→ 24→There is no error, no warning — you simply get fewer results than expected. This makes the bug difficult to detect because: 25→ 26→1. Some items may legitimately have both labels, so results aren't empty 27→2. The missing items are invisible — you don't know what you're not seeing 28→3. Tests with small datasets may pass if test items happen to have all labels 29→ 30→## Decision: Query by Type-Specific Labels
... [27 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: GitHub GraphQL API Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "using gh api graphql" 5→ - "writing GraphQL queries for GitHub" 6→ - "passing variables to GraphQL queries" 7→ - "fetching data not available in REST API" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "passing variables to gh api graphql as JSON blob" 10→ warning: "Variables must be passed individually with -f (strings) and -F (typed). The syntax `-f variables={...}` does NOT work." 11→ pattern: "gh\\s+api\\s+graphql" 12→ - action: "passing array or object variables to gh api graphql with -F and json.dumps()" 13→ warning: "Arrays and objects require special gh syntax: arrays use -f key[]=value1 -f key[]=value2, objects use -f key[subkey]=value. Using -F key=[...] or -F key={...} passes them as literal strings, not typed values." 14→ pattern: "json\\.dumps\\(.*-F" 15→last_audited: "2026-02-17 12:00 PT" 16→audit_result: clean 17→--- 18→ 19→# GitHub GraphQL API Patterns 20→ 21→This document describes patterns for using GitHub's GraphQL API via `gh api graphql`. 22→ 23→## When to Use GraphQL vs REST 24→ 25→**Use GraphQL when:** 26→ 27→- Data is only available via GraphQL (e.g., PR review thread resolution status) 28→- You need to traverse relationships in a single query 29→- REST would require multiple round trips 30→
... [244 lines omitted] ...
129→- Use `execute_gh_command_with_retry()` (not `execute_gh_command()`) for automatic retry on transient errors 168→If a variable is null, the GraphQL API returns an error like:

1→--- 2→title: Architecture Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on architecture code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from architecture/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Architecture Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**FakePromptExecutor tracks all calls via properties** → Read [Prompt Executor Gateway](prompt-executor-gateway.md) first. use .prompt_calls, .interactive_calls, .passthrough_calls for assertions 16→ 17→**accessing ctx.git or ctx.repo without checking for NoRepoSentinel** → Read [No-Repo Infrastructure](no-repo-infrastructure.md) first. Commands decorated with @no_repo_required may have sentinel gateways. Check isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) before repo operations. 18→ 19→**accessing ctx.obj directly without a require\_\*() helper** → Read [Click Context Dependency Injection Pattern](click-context-di-pattern.md) first. Use typed require\_\*() helpers (require_issues, require_git, require_cwd, etc.) instead of direct ctx.obj access. Helpers provide type narrowing and clear error messages. 20→ 21→**accessing plan_ref.pr_id as int without checking** → Read [PlanRef Architecture](plan-ref-architecture.md) first. pr_id is a string. Use LBYL: `plan_ref.pr_id.isdigit()` before `int(plan_ref.pr_id)`. Supports future non-numeric providers like 'PROJ-123'. 22→ 23→**accessing properties on a discriminated union result without isinstance() check** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Always check isinstance(result, ErrorType) before accessing success-variant properties. Without type narrowing, you may access .message on a success type or .data on an error type. 24→ 25→**adding HTML, badges, or GitHub-specific markup to commit messages** → Read [PR Body Formatting Pattern](pr-body-formatting.md) first. Use the two-target pattern: plain text pr_body for commits, enhanced pr_body_for_github for the PR. Never put GitHub-specific HTML into git commit messages. 26→ 27→**adding LBYL guards that only check a subset of required fields** → Read [Erk Architecture Patterns](erk-architecture.md) first. Functions with 'skip silently' contracts must validate ALL preconditions with LBYL guards. Checking one field (e.g., schema_version) is insufficient — use set operations to verify all required fields. 28→ 29→**adding LLM-dependent logic inside a Click @command function in exec/scripts/** → Read [Inference Hoisting Pattern](inference-hoisting.md) first. Inference hoisting violation: exec scripts run as subprocesses; they cannot nest LLM calls within a Claude Code session. Move reasoning to the calling skill. 30→
... [389 lines omitted] ...
45→**adding a new dispatch handler that does not return (branch_name, run_id)** → Read [Unified Dispatch Pattern](unified-dispatch-pattern.md) first. All PR-targeting dispatch handlers must return (branch_name, run_id) for post-dispatch metadata enrichment. learn and consolidate-learn-plans are exceptions (no branch/run_id needed for metadata). See unified-dispatch-pattern.md. 47→**adding a new field to ErkContext dataclass** → Read [Erk Architecture Patterns](erk-architecture.md) first. Update ALL factory functions. Grep: `grep -r 'ErkContext(' packages/erk-shared/src/ src/erk/core/context.py` to find all construction sites. Missing a factory causes runtime errors or silent None values. 65→**adding a validation gate without actionable feedback in the error message** → Read [Agent Back Pressure via Gates](agent-backpressure-gates.md) first. Gates must include the expected pattern, the actual value, and examples so the agent can self-correct. See InvalidObjectiveSlug.message for the pattern. 87→**assuming GitHub API failures are transient without repository-specific testing** → Read [GitHub API Diagnostics](github-api-diagnostics.md) first. Test with a control repository first. Some GitHub bugs affect specific repos but not others. Follow the 3-step diagnostic methodology. 95→**bypassing PrListService for direct GitHub API plan queries** → Read [HTTP-Accelerated Plan Refresh](http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md) first. PrListService handles HTTP API calls. Direct API calls bypass caching and error handling. 107→**calling checkout_branch() in a multi-worktree repository** → Read [Multi-Worktree State Handling](multi-worktree-state.md) first. Verify the target branch is not already checked out in another worktree using `git.worktree.find_worktree_for_branch()`. Git enforces a single-checkout constraint - attempting to checkout a branch held elsewhere causes silent state corruption or unexpected failures. 121→**calling execute_gh_command() instead of execute_gh_command_with_retry() for network-sensitive operations** → Read [GitHub API Retry Mechanism](github-api-retry-mechanism.md) first. Use `execute_gh_command_with_retry()` for operations that may fail due to transient network errors. Pass `time_impl` for testability. 123→**calling get_X() and handling IssueNotFound sentinel inline** → Read [LBYL Gateway Pattern](lbyl-gateway-pattern.md) first. Check with X_exists() first for cleaner error messages and LBYL compliance. 133→**calling os.chdir() in erk code** [pattern: `os\.chdir\(`] → Read [Erk Architecture Patterns](erk-architecture.md) first. After os.chdir(), regenerate context using regenerate_context(ctx). Stale ctx.cwd causes FileNotFoundError. 143→**changing a gateway method signature** → Read [Gateway Signature Migration](gateway-signature-migration.md) first. Search for ALL callers with grep before changing. PR #6329 migrated 8 call sites across 7 files. Missing a call site causes runtime errors. 147→**changing erk_shared function signatures** → Read [Gateway Signature Migration](gateway-signature-migration.md) first. Grep all callers across full repo before committing. Missed call sites cause CI failures. 161→**choosing between exceptions and discriminated unions for operation failures** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. If callers branch on the error and continue the operation, use discriminated unions. If all callers just terminate and surface the message, use exceptions. Read the 'When to Use' section. 177→**creating Protocol with bare attributes for frozen dataclasses** → Read [Protocol vs ABC Interface Design Guide](protocol-vs-abc.md) first. Use @property decorators in Protocol for frozen dataclass compatibility. Bare attributes cause type errors. 183→**creating a new complex command with multiple validation steps** → Read [Linear Pipeline Architecture](linear-pipelines.md) first. Consider two-pipeline pattern: validation pipeline (check preconditions) + execution pipeline (perform operations). Use discriminated unions (State | Error) for pipeline steps. Reference land_pipeline.py as exemplar. 199→**designing error handling for a new gateway method** → Read [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) first. Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section. 239→**migrating a gateway method to return discriminated union** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Update all implementations (ABC, real, fake; add dry_run only for dry-run-participating gateways) AND all call sites AND tests. Incomplete migrations break type safety. 241→**migrating git method calls after subgateway extraction** → Read [Gateway Decomposition Phases](gateway-decomposition-phases.md) first. The following methods have been moved from the Git ABC to subgateways: `git.fetch_branch()` → `git.remote.fetch_branch()` (Phase 3), `git.push_to_remote()` → `git.remote.push_to_remote()` (Phase 3), `git.commit()` → `git.commit.commit()` (Phase 4), `git.stage_files()` → `git.commit.stage_files()` (Phase 4), `git.has_staged_changes()` → `git.status.has_staged_changes()` (Phase 5), `git.rebase_onto()` → `git.rebase.rebase_onto()` (Phase 6), `git.tag_exists()` → `git.tag_exists()` (Phase 7), `git.create_tag()` → `git.tag.create_tag()` (Phase 7). Calling the old API will raise `AttributeError`. Always use the subgateway property. 243→**mixing discriminated unions with exception-based cleanup in a single method** → Read [Mixed Exception/Union Pattern (Deprecated)](gateway-specific-patterns.md) first. This pattern was tried in PR #6347 and reverted in PR #6375. Message-only discriminated unions with no domain-meaningful variants add complexity without value. Use exceptions for all error cases, or use discriminated unions with meaningful variants throughout. Document why in the PR. 245→**modifying Claude CLI error reporting or PromptResult.error format** → Read [Claude CLI Error Reporting](claude-cli-error-reporting.md) first. Error messages must maintain structured format with exit code, stderr, and context. Changes affect all callers of execute_prompt() and execute_command_streaming(). 259→**parsing CalledProcessError messages for git operations** → Read [Git Operation Patterns](git-operation-patterns.md) first. Avoid parsing git error messages to determine failure modes. Use LBYL with git show-ref --verify to check existence before operations, or design discriminated unions that handle all returncode cases explicitly. 261→**passing None for http_client in service methods** → Read [HTTP-Accelerated Plan Refresh](http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md) first. http_client is a required parameter (not optional) in PrListService and ObjectiveListService. Passing None causes TypeError. Validate at CLI entry point. 275→**placing @handle_non_ideal_exit before @click.command() or @click.pass_context** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. @handle_non_ideal_exit must come AFTER @click.command() / @click.pass_context (outermost position in decorator stack). Inner position causes it to receive wrong arguments. 295→**removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC** → Read [Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist](gateway-abc-implementation.md) first. Must remove from all implementation files simultaneously (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites. Verify with grep across packages. 297→**returning a collection (list, tuple, str) directly from a function with NonIdealState return type** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Raw built-ins can't inherit EnsurableResult. Wrap in a named frozen dataclass that inherits EnsurableResult and implements **iter** if needed. See 'The Wrapping Rule' section. 309→**suppressing F401 (unused import) warnings** → Read [Re-Export Pattern](re-export-pattern.md) first. Use # noqa: F401 comment per-import with reason, not global ruff config. Indicates intentional re-export vs actual unused import. 311→**swallowing errors silently in a deterministic code path** → Read [Error Handling Patterns](error-handling-patterns.md) first. Never suppress errors deterministically. Use best-effort (catch + log warning) only for truly optional operations. Use error-first (check + fail with remediation) for required operations. 319→**try/except in fake.py or dry_run.py** [pattern: `\btry:|\bexcept\s`] → Read [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) first. Gateway error handling (try/except) belongs ONLY in real.py. Fake and dry-run implementations return error discriminants based on constructor params, they don't catch exceptions. 327→**using None as a success return value in a validation function (ErrorType | None where None = success)** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. None-as-success is counterintuitive — None typically signals absence/failure, not success. Use ValidThing | InvalidThing so both outcomes are explicit named types. 331→**using PrContextProvider** → Read [Plan Context Integration](plan-context-integration.md) first. Read this doc first. PrContextProvider returns None on any failure (graceful degradation). Always handle the None case. 333→**using Protocol property descriptors on a frozen dataclass NonIdealState class** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Protocol property descriptors conflict with frozen dataclass fields. Use NonIdealStateMixin (at packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py) when the NonIdealState class uses dataclass fields. 337→**using `gt restack` to resolve branch divergence errors** [pattern: `gt\s+restack`] → Read [Git and Graphite Edge Cases Catalog](git-graphite-quirks.md) first. gt restack only handles parent-child stack rebasing, NOT same-branch remote divergence. Use git rebase origin/$BRANCH first. 339→**using bare subprocess.run with check=True** [pattern: `subprocess\.run\(`] → Read [Subprocess Wrappers](subprocess-wrappers.md) first. Use wrapper functions: run_subprocess_with_context() (gateway) or run_with_error_reporting() (CLI). Exception: Graceful degradation pattern with explicit CalledProcessError handling is acceptable for optional operations. 361→**using gh pr view --json merged** [pattern: `gh\s+pr\s+view.*--json.*\bmerged\b`] → Read [GitHub API Rate Limits](github-api-rate-limits.md) first. The `merged` field doesn't exist. Use `mergedAt` instead. Run `gh pr view --help` or check error output for valid field names. 363→**using git merge on a Graphite-managed branch** → Read [Git and Graphite Edge Cases Catalog](git-graphite-quirks.md) first. Merge commits break Graphite's linear stack model. Use git pull --rebase or gt sync. Merge commits cause gt squash divergence errors and broken parent tracking. 367→**using git stash in scripts that have running processes dependent on working tree state** → Read [Git Operation Patterns](git-operation-patterns.md) first. git stash changes working tree state which affects running processes. If code is executing from the working tree (e.g., Python scripts), stashing can cause import errors or missing file errors in the running process. 369→**using if/else on a discriminated union without isinstance() for type narrowing** → Read [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md) first. Type checkers require isinstance() for narrowing. 'if result.is_error' or 'if not result' does not narrow. Use 'if isinstance(result, ErrorType):' for correct narrowing in both branches. 391→**using try/except KeyError for JSON field access** → Read [JSON Parsing Patterns](json-parsing-patterns.md) first. Use LBYL pattern: check field presence with `any(f not in data for f in _REQUIRED_FIELDS)` before accessing. Never use try/except for control flow. 405→**writing a try-except block that wraps multiple independent operations** → Read [Error Handling Patterns](error-handling-patterns.md) first. Minimal exception scope: each try block should wrap only the single operation that can raise the caught exception. Split into separate try-except blocks with early returns. Broader scope is only acceptable when statements form an atomic unit (same message and recovery regardless of which line raises). See dignified-python references/exception-handling.md. 407→**writing complex business logic directly in Click command functions** → Read [CLI-to-Pipeline Boundary Pattern](cli-to-pipeline-boundary.md) first. Extract to pipeline layer when command has >3 distinct steps or complex state management. CLI layer should handle: Click decorators, parameter parsing, output formatting. Pipeline layer should handle: business logic, state management, error types. 409→**writing manual JSON serialization/deserialization for exec scripts** → Read [JSON/Dataclass Utilities in erk-shared](json-dataclass-utilities.md) first. Use @json_command or the utilities in erk_shared.agentclick.dataclass_json instead. They handle schema generation, type coercion, and error formatting automatically.
session.assistant: Now I have enough context. Let me read the remaining skill and command files to complete my review. Let me read the last batch of files:
session.tool_result:
1→# Objective Workflow Reference 2→ 3→Detailed procedures for creating objectives, spawning plans, and resuming work. 4→ 5→## Contents 6→ 7→- [Creating a New Objective](#creating-a-new-objective) 8→- [Spawning Erk-Plans](#spawning-erk-plans) 9→- [Resuming Work on an Objective](#resuming-work-on-an-objective) 10→- [Structuring for Steelthread Development](#structuring-for-steelthread-development) 11→- [Best Practices](#best-practices) 12→ 13→For updating and closing objectives, see: 14→ 15→- [updating.md](updating.md) - Two-step update workflow 16→- [closing.md](closing.md) - Closing triggers and procedures 17→ 18→## Creating a New Objective 19→ 20→### When to Create 21→ 22→Create an objective when: 23→ 24→- A goal requires 2+ related PRs to complete 25→- Work spans multiple sessions or days 26→- Lessons learned should be captured for future reference 27→- Coordination across related changes is needed 28→ 29→Do NOT create an objective for: 30→
... [232 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→name: objective 3→description: > 4→ This skill should be used when managing objectives - coherent goals that require 2+ plans/PRs 5→ to complete. Use when users want to create multi-plan tracking documents, log progress across 6→ related PRs, capture lessons learned, or coordinate work that spans multiple erk-plans. 7→ Objectives are human-first coordination documents, not executable plans. 8→--- 9→ 10→# Objective Skill 11→ 12→## Overview 13→ 14→Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike 15→erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and 16→capture lessons learned along the way. 17→ 18→**Scope range:** 19→ 20→- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs 21→- Medium: Refactor spanning several plans 22→- Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans 23→ 24→## Objective vs Erk-Plan 25→ 26→| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | 27→| -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | 28→| Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | 29→| Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | 30→| Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown |
... [174 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→name: erk-exec 3→description: > 4→ Guide for erk exec subcommands. Use when running erk exec commands 5→ to understand syntax, find the right command for a task, or learn 6→ common workflows. Always check syntax with -h or load this skill 7→ before running erk exec commands. 8→--- 9→ 10→# erk exec Guide 11→ 12→## Quick Start 13→ 14→Before running any `erk exec` command, check syntax with `-h`: 15→ 16→```bash 17→erk exec <command> -h 18→``` 19→ 20→## Commands by Workflow 21→ 22→### PR Review Operations 23→ 24→When addressing PR review comments or resolving threads: 25→ 26→| Command | Purpose | 27→| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | 28→| `get-pr-review-comments` | Fetch review comments (use `--pr N`) | 29→| `resolve-review-thread` | Resolve a thread (use `--thread-id`) | 30→| `reply-to-discussion-comment` | Reply to discussion comment |
... [55 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→description: Create an implementation plan for a known objective node (inner skill) 3→argument-hint: "<objective-number> --node <node-id>" 4→allowed-tools: Bash, Task, Skill, AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode 5→--- 6→ 7→# /erk:system:objective-plan-node 8→ 9→Inner skill for creating an implementation plan when the objective issue and node ID are already known. Called by the outer `/erk:objective-plan` command or directly by `plan_cmd.py` via Claude launch. 10→ 11→This skips interactive node selection (Steps 4-5 of the outer command) since the node is already determined. 12→ 13→## Usage 14→ 15→```bash 16→/erk:system:objective-plan-node 3679 --node 2.1 17→``` 18→ 19→Both `<objective-number>` and `--node <node-id>` are required. 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Agent Instructions 24→ 25→### Step 1: Parse Arguments 26→ 27→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` to extract: 28→ 29→- **Objective number**: The numeric objective reference (required) 30→- **Node ID**: The `--node` value (required)
... [181 lines omitted] ...
32→If either is missing, STOP and report: "ERROR: Both objective number and --node are required. Usage: /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <node-id>" 51→ - If 'erk-plan' label instead: return error "This is an erk-plan PR, not an objective" 52→ - If neither label: include warning but proceed 73→WARNINGS: <any warnings about labels, roadmap format, etc., or "none"> 96→"ERROR: objective-context marker not created. Re-run the marker command manually: 186→## Error Cases 190→| Missing objective number or node ID | Report error with usage instructions | 191→| Objective not found | Report error and exit | 193→| Node not found in roadmap | Report error and list available nodes | 194→| Marker creation fails | Report error with manual command |

1→--- 2→description: Audit open plans for staleness and validity 3→--- 4→ 5→# /local:audit-plans 6→ 7→Audits open plans to identify stale or completed plans that may need attention or closing. 8→ 9→## Usage 10→ 11→```bash 12→/local:audit-plans # Oldest 20 plans 13→/local:audit-plans --all # All open plans 14→``` 15→ 16→--- 17→ 18→## Agent Instructions 19→ 20→### Phase 1: List Open Plans 21→ 22→Fetch all open plans, sorted oldest first: 23→ 24→```bash 25→gh api repos/dagster-io/erk/issues \ 26→ -X GET \ 27→ --paginate \ 28→ -f labels=erk-pr,erk-plan \ 29→ -f state=open \ 30→ -f per_page=100 \
... [195 lines omitted] ...
83→> **WARNING:** `impl_event: ended` only means an implementation attempt ended, NOT that it succeeded. 109→| No PR found, no changes in codebase | **Incomplete** - impl failed or PR not merged | 179→Report results and any failures. 206→## Error Handling

1→--- 2→description: Analyze and prioritize open PRs for stability-focused landing order 3→--- 4→ 5→# /local:pr-triage 6→ 7→Analyzes all open PRs, classifies them into priority tiers, and recommends a stability-focused landing order. 8→ 9→## Usage 10→ 11→```bash 12→/local:pr-triage 13→``` 14→ 15→--- 16→ 17→## Agent Instructions 18→ 19→### Phase 1: Data Collection 20→ 21→Fetch all open PRs with metadata: 22→ 23→```bash 24→gh pr list --state open --limit 100 --json number,title,labels,isDraft,additions,deletions,changedFiles,statusCheckRollup,updatedAt,createdAt,author 25→``` 26→ 27→Store the result for analysis. 28→ 29→### Phase 2: Classify Each PR 30→
... [63 lines omitted] ...
49→ - FAIL: any check has conclusion "FAILURE" or "CANCELLED"

1→--- 2→description: Create a structured objective through guided conversation 3→--- 4→ 5→# /erk:objective-create 6→ 7→Create a new objective through an interactive, guided process. You describe what you want to accomplish, and Claude proposes a structured objective for your approval. 8→ 9→## Usage 10→ 11→```bash 12→/erk:objective-create 13→``` 14→ 15→--- 16→ 17→## Agent Instructions 18→ 19→### Step 1: Prompt for Description 20→ 21→Ask the user to describe what they want to accomplish: 22→ 23→``` 24→What do you want to accomplish? Describe it however makes sense to you - goals, 25→constraints, design decisions you've already made, context about the codebase, etc. 26→ 27→I'll structure it into a formal objective for your review. 28→``` 29→ 30→Wait for the user's response. They may provide:
... [334 lines omitted] ...
351→## Error Cases 356→| Not authenticated | Report GitHub auth error | 357→| Issue creation fails | Report API error, offer to retry | 358→| Plan file write fails | Report error with path |

1→--- 2→description: Create an implementation plan from an objective node 3→argument-hint: "<objective-number-or-url> [--node <node-id>]" 4→allowed-tools: Bash, Skill, AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode 5→--- 6→ 7→# /erk:objective-plan 8→ 9→Create an implementation plan for a specific node in an objective's dependency graph. 10→ 11→## Usage 12→ 13→```bash 14→/erk:objective-plan 3679 15→/erk:objective-plan 3679 --node 2.1 16→/erk:objective-plan https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/3679 17→/erk:objective-plan # prompts for objective reference 18→``` 19→ 20→--- 21→ 22→## Agent Instructions 23→ 24→### Step 0: Check for Known Node (Fast Path) 25→ 26→Parse `$ARGUMENTS` for `--node <node-id>`. If `--node` is present along with an issue number: 27→ 28→1. Run `/erk:objective-reconcile <objective-number>` via the Skill tool. 29→2. Then invoke the inner skill via the Skill tool: 30→
... [133 lines omitted] ...
63→- If `"success": false` with `"error": "not_found"`: Report error and exit. 64→- If `"success": false` with `"error": "is_plan"`: Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement`. 65→- If `"success": false` with `"error": "validation_error"`: Report error and exit. 68→Display any `"warnings"` to the user. 135→## Error Cases 139→| Objective not found | Report error and exit |

1→--- 2→description: Replan an existing plan against current codebase state 3→argument-hint: <pr-number-or-url> 4→--- 5→ 6→# /erk:replan 7→ 8→Recomputes existing plan(s) against the current codebase state, creating a new plan and closing the original(s). 9→ 10→Supports consolidating multiple plans into a single unified plan. 11→ 12→## Usage 13→ 14→```bash 15→/erk:replan 2521 # Single plan replan 16→/erk:replan https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 17→/erk:replan 123 456 789 # Consolidate multiple plans 18→``` 19→ 20→--- 21→ 22→## Agent Instructions 23→ 24→### Step 1: Parse Plan References 25→ 26→Split `$ARGUMENTS` on whitespace. For each argument: 27→ 28→- If numeric (e.g., `2521`), use directly as pr number 29→- If URL (e.g., `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521`), extract the number from the path 30→
... [476 lines omitted] ...
65→> ERRORS: 66→> <only present if VALIDATION is FAIL, list each error> 68→> WARNINGS: 69→> <only present if any issues are closed, list each warning> 82→> - PASS otherwise (closed plans are warnings, not failures) 86→1. **If VALIDATION is FAIL**: Display each error and abort: 89→ Error: PR #<number> is not a valid plan (missing both erk-plan and erk-learn labels). 93→ Error: PR #<number> not found. 96→2. **If WARNINGS present**: Display each warning but continue: 99→ Warning: PR #<number> is already closed. Proceeding with replan anyway. 146→Return an error to the main agent: 149→Error: PR #<number> not found. 481→## Error Cases 483→| Error | Message | 485→| Plan not found | `Error: PR #<number> not found.` | 486→| Not a plan | `Error: PR #<number> is not a valid plan (missing both erk-plan and erk-learn labels).` | 487→| No plan content | `Error: No plan content found in PR #<number>.` | 488→| GitHub CLI not available | `Error: GitHub CLI (gh) not available. Run: brew install gh && gh auth login` | 489→| No network | `Error: Unable to reach GitHub. Check network connectivity.` |
